# SESSION BRIEF · BU-comments — Post detail page + flat comment thread

_Brief version: 1.0 · Author: Paul + Claude · Date: 2026-04-26_
_Priority: Phase 2 demo-leverage. Direct successor to BU-reactions._
_Pairs with: BU-reactions (the Reaction primitive's polymorphic
shape extends to support `comment` as a target); D050._

---

## Objective

Members can read and write comments on any post via a new post-
detail page (`/post/[id]`). The post anchors near the top, the
discussion thread stacks underneath, an in-line composer sits at
the bottom. Default filter "Discussion"; toggles for "Activity"
and "All". Per Scenario 20.

Success: Eddie (per SCN-20) opens Maya's Sky News post from the
feed, reads the discussion (Sharon's reply, David T's "took 90
seconds", a coordinator-pinned answer), takes the AM action,
scrolls back to the composer, types a thank-you reply, taps Post.
His comment lands at the bottom of the Discussion thread with his
display name + "new member" chip + relative timestamp.

---

## Scope

### Build in this session

**Decision record (must land before schema migration):**

- `docs/architecture/decision-log.md` — append `D052 · Comment
  schema + polymorphic reuse of ReactionTargetType` (MODIFY,
  append-only)

**Schema:**

- `prisma/schema.prisma` (MODIFY — append `Comment` model; extend
  the `ReactionTargetType` enum to add `comment`; add `commentId?`
  FK on `Reaction` with cascade. Add `comments Comment[]` relation
  back-refs on `Post` and `User`. Do NOT modify any existing fields
  on Post, User, or Reaction.)
- `prisma/migrations/<timestamp>_add_comments/migration.sql`
  (generated by `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_comments`)

**Service:**

- `server/services/comment.ts` (new) — `createComment`,
  `listCommentsForPost` (oldest-first, soft-delete-respecting,
  visibility-respecting at the parent-post level)
- `server/services/comment.README.md` (new)

**Router:**

- `server/routers/comment.ts` (new) — `add`, `listForPost`
- `server/routers/_app.ts` (MODIFY — register `comment` router
  alongside `reaction`; preserve all other entries)

**Validation:**

- `shared/validation/comment.ts` (new) — Zod schemas for `add` and
  `listForPost`. Body min 1, max 5000 characters; postId UUID.

**Feed integration (source-card hook):**

- `server/services/post.ts` (MODIFY — extend `listPosts` return
  shape with `commentCount: number` per post, fetched via a single
  `groupBy postId` aggregate (avoids N+1, mirrors the
  reactions-bulk pattern). Existing fields preserved exactly; new
  field appended.)
- `components/PostCard.tsx` (MODIFY — render a "💬 N comments"
  affordance below the body when `commentCount > 0`. Wrap the
  whole card in a Next.js `<Link href={\`/post/${id}\`}>` so
  tap-card-to-detail works. Reaction pill remains tap-isolated
  via `e.stopPropagation`.)

**Page:**

- `app/post/[id]/page.tsx` (new) — server component. Loads post,
  reactions, and comments via `createCaller(ctx)`. Returns 404 on
  not-found / soft-deleted. Returns gated landing on
  `members_only` posts when caller unauthed (per D045). Renders
  the post anchored near top + the discussion thread + the
  composer. The route is dynamic, deep-linkable per D045.
- `app/post/[id]/actions.ts` (new) — server action
  `addCommentAction(postId, body)` that calls
  `caller.comment.add`.

**UI:**

- `components/CommentList.tsx` (new) — client component;
  receives initial comments + the add server action. Renders
  filter tabs (Discussion / Activity / All — the latter two
  rendered but no system comments exist yet, so they're empty
  states). Renders the list. Renders `<CommentComposer />` at
  the bottom.
- `components/CommentItem.tsx` (new) — single comment render:
  author display name, role chips (existing), "new member" chip
  if the author was created within the last 14 days, body, and
  relative timestamp. No edit / delete UI in MVP.
- `components/CommentComposer.tsx` (new) — single-line tap-to-
  expand composer at the bottom of the thread. Optimistic insert
  via React 19 `useOptimistic`, with the same local-commit
  pattern as `ReactionPill` (see fix in PR #47 — the optimistic
  state must survive transition completion).

**F14 area prefix:**

- `eslint-rules/canonical-areas.json` (MODIFY — add `comment` to
  areas array; preserve order and the existing entries)
- `docs/process/testid-convention.md` (MODIFY — add `comment` row
  to the area table)

**Audit:**

- `server/services/audit.ts` is NOT modified. The comment service
  calls existing `auditLog` writer with `action: 'comment.add'`,
  `entityType: 'comment'`. No schema or function changes.

**Analytics:**

- `comment_added` event already specced in
  `docs/product/analytics-events.md`. Service layer fires it on
  successful `createComment` (mirrors the BU-composer pattern of
  silent-for-now until an analytics writer lands). No changes to
  the analytics doc.

**Tests:**

- `tests/integration/comment-service.test.ts` (new, 5+ cases) —
  create, list, soft-delete-respecting, visibility-respecting,
  cascade on user delete
- `tests/unit/comment-router.test.ts` (new, 5+ cases) — auth gate,
  flag gate, Zod rejection, list shape, system-comment field
- Existing `post-list` and `post-service` tests updated to mock
  the new `prisma.comment` table (mirrors the reactions test
  update from PR #46)

**Seed:**

- `scripts/seed.ts` (MODIFY) — seed `ff_comments` flag with
  `enabledGlobally: true`. Seed 3-5 comments per post for
  demoability. Use mix of seeded users; comment bodies short and
  realistic per scenario voice.

**READMEs:**

- `server/services/comment.README.md` (new — already listed)

### Do NOT touch

- Any file not listed above
- Existing `Reaction` service or router behaviour (we extend the
  schema; the existing add/remove/listForPost code paths stay
  identical)
- `Post` model fields, indexes, relations beyond the new
  `comments Comment[]` back-ref
- `User` model fields beyond the `comments Comment[]` back-ref
- `PostType` / `PostVisibility` enums — no changes
- `app/feed/page.tsx` — feed page is unchanged (PostCard
  navigates to `/post/[id]` via a wrapping Link; the page itself
  doesn't need to know about it)
- `app/compose/`, `components/PostForm.tsx`,
  `components/ActivistMailerField.tsx` — no composer changes
- Existing eight F-rules — no rule changes
- `docs/feature-spec/v0.5.docx` — frozen; updates land in v0.6
- `docs/product/scenarios.md` — SCN-20 is the canonical scenario;
  no scenario edits in this session
- The bu-reactions-related code paths (ReactionPill, ReactionTray,
  reaction service/router) — touched only via the extended enum

### Out of scope for this session

- **Reactions on comments.** The schema supports it (`commentId`
  FK on Reaction; `comment` value in `ReactionTargetType`). No UI.
  Lands in a follow-up.
- **Threaded replies.** Flat thread for MVP. Reply-to-comment is
  a future feature; the schema accommodates it later via a
  nullable `parentCommentId` if added.
- **Edit / delete UX.** Out of scope. Authors cannot edit or
  delete their own comments in MVP. Audit log records `add`; no
  `edit` / `remove` actions yet. Lands with BU-admin or a
  dedicated follow-up.
- **@mentions.** No mention parsing, no autocomplete, no
  notifications.
- **Rich text / markdown / images.** Plain text only.
  `\n\n` paragraph splits respected like Post body, per the
  PostCard precedent.
- **System / auto-comments.** The "Activity" filter renders an
  empty state; system comments (verified, pinned, region-corrected,
  threshold-reached) land with BU-dispatch / BU-flag /
  BU-outcome-review.
- **Notifications on receiving a comment.** Per design-philosophy
  principle 3.
- **Pagination on long threads.** Load all for MVP (typical
  thread <50). Infinite scroll / cursor pagination is a future
  polish item.
- **Coordinator removal / pinning of comments.** BU-flag /
  BU-admin.
- **Optimistic comment-count update on the source feed card.**
  Lazy refresh OK — count updates on next feed render. Optimistic
  cross-component updates are a polish item.
- **Cross-tab realtime.** Same as BU-reactions; out of scope.
- **Comment-level visibility.** All comments inherit the parent
  post's visibility. No per-comment visibility flags.

---

## Contracts

### Inputs consumed

- `Post` model (read-only — only the relation back-ref is added)
- `User` model — only the relation back-ref is added
- `Reaction` model — `commentId` FK and `comment` enum value added;
  existing rows untouched
- `auditLog` from `server/services/audit.ts` — call signature
  unchanged
- `isFeatureEnabled` from `server/services/flags.ts` — gate `add`
- `ctx.user` from `server/routers/context.ts`
- `authedProcedure` / `publicProcedure` from `server/lib/trpc.ts`
- `listReactionsForPost` and feed-shape contract from D050 (the
  detail page renders reactions on the post, same as feed cards)

### Outputs produced

**Schema additions (the contract — needs D052):**

```prisma
enum ReactionTargetType {
  post
  comment  // ← new value (BU-comments / D052)
}

model Comment {
  id String @id @default(uuid())

  postId String
  post   Post   @relation(fields: [postId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  authorId String
  author   User   @relation("commentAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)

  body String

  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
  deletedAt DateTime?

  reactions Reaction[] @relation("commentReactions")

  @@index([postId, createdAt])
  @@index([authorId, createdAt])
  @@index([deletedAt])
}

model Reaction {
  // existing fields preserved
  commentId String?
  comment   Comment? @relation("commentReactions", fields: [commentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  // existing @@unique and indexes preserved exactly
}
```

**Why `onDelete: Restrict` on Comment.author:** consistent with
`Post.author` — a user with comments cannot be hard-deleted; soft-
delete preserves community history.

**Why `onDelete: Cascade` on Comment.post:** if a post is hard-
deleted (rare; posts use soft-delete), its comments go with it.
Cascade prevents orphans.

**Router contract (the API):**

```ts
// server/routers/comment.ts

comment.add: authedProcedure
  .input(z.object({ postId: z.string().uuid(), body: z.string().min(1).max(5000) }))
  .mutation: returns { id: string } | throws TRPCError

comment.listForPost: publicProcedure
  .input(z.object({ postId: z.string().uuid() }))
  .query: returns CommentListItem[]
```

`comment.add` is gated by `ff_comments` (FORBIDDEN if off). Auth via
`authedProcedure`. The body is trimmed before persistence (leading
/ trailing whitespace removed; internal preserved).

`CommentListItem` shape:

```ts
{
  id: string;
  body: string;
  createdAt: Date;
  author: {
    id: string;
    displayName: string;
    roles: SystemRole[];
    isNewMember: boolean;  // true if account < 14 days old
  };
}
```

Sort: `createdAt ASC` (oldest first — chronological reading order
per SCN-20).

**Feed-page contract (the integration point):**

`listPosts` returns each post with an additional field:

```ts
{
  ...existingFields,
  commentCount: number;  // 0 when none; non-deleted only
}
```

`reactions` field already exists from D050.

---

## Acceptance criteria

### Functional

- [ ] D052 ADR appended to decision-log.md
- [ ] Schema migration runs cleanly on a fresh DB and on a DB with
      existing seeded posts + reactions (no rows lost)
- [ ] `Comment` model exists with all listed fields and indexes
- [ ] `ReactionTargetType` enum has both `post` and `comment` values
- [ ] `Reaction.commentId` column exists and cascades on
      Comment delete
- [ ] `comment.add` creates a Comment row, fires `comment_added`
      analytics event, returns `{ id }` on success
- [ ] `comment.add` rejects unauth callers with `UNAUTHORIZED`
- [ ] `comment.add` rejects when `ff_comments` is off (`FORBIDDEN`)
- [ ] `comment.add` rejects body of length 0 / > 5000 / non-UUID
      postId via Zod
- [ ] `comment.listForPost` returns oldest-first; soft-deleted
      excluded; visibility-respecting at the parent post level
      (a non-authed caller sees comments on `public` posts only,
      gated landing on `members_only`, 404 on `private`)
- [ ] `post.list` returns `commentCount` on every post
- [ ] `/post/[id]` renders the post anchored near the top with
      reactions and AM URL preserved, then the discussion thread
      below, then the composer
- [ ] PostCard wraps in a Link to `/post/[id]`; reaction pill
      taps don't trigger navigation (event-isolated)
- [ ] Comment composer expands on tap, accepts text, posts on
      submit, optimistically appears at the bottom
- [ ] Optimistic comment survives the transition (uses the
      committed-state pattern from PR #47, not the prop-only
      pattern that broke in PR #46)
- [ ] "New member" chip appears on Eddie's first comment per
      SCN-20 (account < 14 days old)
- [ ] Filter tabs (Discussion / Activity / All) render. Discussion
      is default; Activity and All render empty states (no system
      comments exist yet — that's BU-dispatch/flag territory)
- [ ] All testids on new interactive elements use the `comment-`
      area prefix per F14

### Non-functional

- [ ] TypeScript compiles with zero errors, zero `any`, zero
      `@ts-ignore`
- [ ] All existing tests pass (`npm run test`)
- [ ] New tests pass (5+ integration, 5+ unit minimum)
- [ ] Lint passes (`npm run lint`) — every new file has
      `@build-unit BU-comments` AND meaningful `@spec` per F13
- [ ] Boundaries respected per F06
- [ ] No PII in any log line touched by this BU
- [ ] Manual click-through of Scenario 20 completed in dev — log
      in as Eddie (or a seeded user), tap any seeded post, read
      the thread, write a comment, refresh → comment persists
- [ ] Manual regression check of Scenario 18 (compose) and
      Scenario 3 (reactions) — neither breaks
- [ ] `npx prisma format` applied; migration committed to git
- [ ] Commit message: `feat(comments): BU-comments — post detail
      page + flat comment thread`

### What "ready to merge" looks like

- All acceptance criteria pass
- F14 lint clean (every new interactive element has a testid)
- Manual scenario click-through screenshot or short video on PR
- D052 in decision-log.md
- READMEs (`server/services/comment.README.md`) explain the
  service surface
- Open-questions section populated with anything that came up
  during build

## Permission matrix

| Action                          | Public       | Member | Author | Coordinator | Director |
| ------------------------------- | ------------ | ------ | ------ | ----------- | -------- |
| View comments on `public` post  | ✓            | ✓      | ✓      | ✓           | ✓        |
| View comments on `members_only` | gated landing| ✓      | ✓      | ✓           | ✓        |
| View comments on `private`      | 404          | ✓ (if author) | ✓ | ✓           | ✓        |
| Add comment to any post         | —            | ✓      | ✓      | ✓           | ✓        |
| Edit own comment                | (out of scope) | (out of scope) | (out of scope) | (out of scope) | (out of scope) |
| Delete own comment              | (out of scope) | (out of scope) | (out of scope) | (out of scope) | (out of scope) |
| Remove someone's comment        | —            | —      | —      | —           | —        |
| Pin a comment                   | —            | —      | —      | —           | —        |

`comment.add` uses `authedProcedure`. `comment.listForPost` uses
`publicProcedure` and applies parent-post visibility filter via
the same logic that exists in `listPosts`. No inline auth checks
per F06 rule 4.

Edit / delete / remove / pin all explicitly out of MVP. Surface in
open questions if any of these feel demo-breaking.

---

## UI states

### Post detail page

| State                          | Trigger                              | What user sees                                                                  |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Post not found / soft-deleted  | Invalid id / deletedAt set           | 404 page                                                                        |
| Post `private`, caller unauthed| Visibility check fails               | 404 (indistinguishable from deleted, per D045)                                  |
| Post `members_only`, unauthed  | Visibility check fails               | Gated landing — "Sign in to see this post" link to `/dev/login?returnTo=...`    |
| Post visible, no comments      | Empty thread                         | Post + "Discussion" filter active + "No replies yet" + composer                 |
| Post visible, with comments    | Thread populated                     | Post + thread oldest-first + composer at bottom                                 |
| Activity filter selected       | Tab clicked                          | Empty state ("No system events on this post yet") — placeholder for BU-dispatch |
| All filter selected            | Tab clicked                          | Same as Discussion in MVP (no system comments exist)                            |

### Comment composer

| State                          | Trigger                              | What user sees                                                                  |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Collapsed                      | Default                              | Single line: "Add a comment…"                                                   |
| Expanded                       | Tap                                  | Textarea with focus, Post button (disabled until non-empty), Cancel button      |
| Submitting (optimistic)        | Post tapped                          | Comment appears at bottom of thread immediately; mutation in flight             |
| Add failed                     | Server returned error                | Optimistic UI rolls back to last committed state; quiet inline error            |
| Empty after typing-and-deleting| Composer expanded; body now empty    | Silent (no warn). Cancel collapses; outside-tap collapses                       |
| Disabled (flag off)            | `ff_comments` off                    | Composer not rendered; thread renders read-only                                 |

### Comment item

| State                          | Trigger                              | What user sees                                                                  |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Standard                       | Default                              | Author name + role chips + body + relative timestamp                            |
| New member (account < 14 days) | Author created recently              | Same + "new member" chip                                                        |
| Soft-deleted                   | `deletedAt` set                      | Not rendered (filtered out at service layer)                                    |

## Tests required

**Integration (real DB-mocked at boundary):**

1. `createComment` writes a Comment row + audit entry; returns id
2. `listCommentsForPost` returns oldest-first, excludes soft-
   deleted, `mine` not exposed (no concept; comments are public-
   ish within the parent post visibility)
3. Cascade: when a post is hard-deleted, comments cascade
4. Cascade: when a user with comments is hard-deleted, the
   `Restrict` blocks (mirrors Post.author behaviour)
5. Visibility: `listCommentsForPost` returns nothing when caller
   can't see the parent post

**Unit (router):**

6. `comment.add` rejects unauth callers (`UNAUTHORIZED`)
7. `comment.add` rejects when `ff_comments` is off (`FORBIDDEN`)
8. `comment.add` rejects body length 0 / > 5000 via Zod
9. `comment.add` rejects malformed `postId` via Zod
10. `comment.listForPost` returns shape contract correctly

**Existing tests updated:**

11. `tests/unit/post-service.test.ts` mock extended for
    `prisma.comment.groupBy`
12. `tests/integration/post-list.test.ts` mock extended likewise
13. Both should still pass after `commentCount` field is added
    to the return shape

**Manual click-through:**

14. SCN-20 — log in, tap a seeded post, read thread, write a
    comment, verify it appears, refresh, verify persistence

**Not required:**

- Performance benchmarks
- E2E with real browser (no Playwright in this codebase yet)
- Visual-regression
- Accessibility audit beyond keyboard / focus management on the
  composer

## Scenarios to verify against

See `docs/product/scenarios.md`:

- **Scenario 20 (Eddie reads + writes first comment)** — primary
  target. Walks the full read-act-reply flow on the new detail
  page. Click through manually before claiming done.
- **Scenario 1 (Sharon Sky News)** — peripheral. The "tap card to
  open detail" interaction is canonically established here. Verify
  feed-card → detail navigation works for any post, not just the
  one Eddie reads.
- **Scenario 3 (David Shabbat)** — regression. Reactions still
  work on the detail page (the same pill renders both on feed
  cards and on the detail page).

Click through SCN-20 + a regression on SCN-3 + SCN-18 before
claiming done.

---

## Known gotchas

- **PostCard click target.** Wrapping the whole card in `<Link>`
  conflicts with the reaction pill's existing onClick. Solution:
  add `e.stopPropagation()` inside `ReactionPill`'s tap handler
  so the card-level navigation doesn't fire. Same for the AM URL
  link — `e.stopPropagation` so tapping the AM button opens AM,
  not the detail page.
- **Optimistic UI gotcha.** Use the committed-state pattern from
  PR #47 (`useState` for committed + `useOptimistic` derived from
  it, with `setCommitted` on success). Don't repeat the
  prop-only `useOptimistic` pattern from PR #46 — that was the
  bug that needed #47 to fix.
- **Migration name collision.** Pull `main` fresh before running
  `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_comments` so the timestamp
  prefix is consistent.
- **Visibility filtering on listForPost.** The simplest path is
  to load the parent post first, fail loudly if not found / not
  visible, then return comments. Don't try to do a join with
  visibility logic embedded — too easy to mis-implement.
- **`isNewMember` definition.** "Account < 14 days old" is a
  simple `createdAt > Date.now() - 14 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000`
  check at service layer. Surface in open questions if 14 days
  is wrong; the brief picks it because Eddie in SCN-20 is "three
  days in".
- **Filter tabs with no system comments.** Activity and All
  render empty states. They exist now so the filter UX is
  established before BU-dispatch / BU-flag start adding system
  comments. Don't try to merge tabs into a single one for MVP —
  the affordance lands now.
- **Body length cap.** 5000 characters is generous (for the
  thoughtful-reply use case from SCN-2). Zod rejects above. UI
  shows a soft hint at 4000 ("a lot of room left") and a hard
  limit at 5000.
- **Cross-tab realtime.** A comment added by user A is not
  visible to user B until B refreshes. Same as reactions.
  Document; do not solve.

## Decisions confirmed before build (Paul, 2026-04-26)

These are locked. The build session executes against them.

1. **Body length cap: 5000** characters (matches Post body upper
   bound). Soft hint at 4000.
2. **`isNewMember` window: 14 days.** Eddie in SCN-20 is "three
   days in"; 14-day window covers the chip use case.
3. **Filter tabs render with empty states.** Discussion / Activity
   / All all render. Activity + All show empty states until system
   comments exist (BU-dispatch / BU-flag / BU-outcome-review).
4. **Author's own comments: no special render.** Role chip already
   differentiates.
5. **Comment count on source card: lazy refresh.** Updates on next
   feed render; no cross-component optimistic.
6. **Soft-delete UX: none in MVP.** Authors cannot edit or delete
   their own comments. Manual DB / admin-patch if urgent.
7. **`/post/[id]` URL slug: UUID.** Pretty slug (`post.shortId`)
   deferred to a future polish session per D045.
8. **`members_only` posts for unauthed callers: gated landing**
   (per D045), not 404. "Sign in to see this post" page.
9. **Activity filter remains empty until system-comment producers
   ship.** Trigger: BU-dispatch / BU-flag / BU-outcome-review
   briefs reference this and add system-comment writes.
10. **PostCard click target: full-card-as-Link.** Reaction pill +
    AM button use `e.stopPropagation` to isolate.

## What this brief does NOT cover

(Naming gaps explicitly, per the discipline.)

1. **Reactions on comments.** Schema-ready; UI is a follow-up.
2. **Threaded replies.** Flat thread for MVP.
3. **Edit / delete UX.** Out.
4. **@mentions, autocomplete, mention notifications.** Out.
5. **Rich text / markdown / images.** Plain text only.
6. **System / auto-comments.** BU-dispatch / BU-flag /
   BU-outcome-review territory.
7. **Notifications on receiving a comment.** Out (principle 3).
8. **Pretty post URL slugs.** D045 scope; future.
9. **Coordinator removal / pinning.** BU-flag / BU-admin.
10. **Comment-level visibility / privacy.** All comments inherit
    the parent post's visibility.
11. **Real-time / cross-tab updates.** Out.
12. **Pagination on long threads.** Future polish.
13. **Read receipts / "X has read your comment".** Out.
14. **Mobile keyboard / IME edge cases.** Default browser
    behaviour; verify on iOS Safari but no special handling.

---

## Slice convention

BU-comments is a **feat** session. Commit type
`feat(comments)`. Establishes:

- The Comment primitive (parent of future threaded replies; reuses
  Reaction polymorphic target)
- The `comment` area prefix in the testid taxonomy
- The post-detail page route as the first dynamic route in the
  app (`/post/[id]`)
- The filter-tab UX (Discussion / Activity / All) — canonicalised
  here so future system-comment work has somewhere to land

Future BUs in this family may include comment-on-comment threading,
edit / delete, attachments on comments, and reactions-on-comments
UI.

---

## What lands after this session

- Members can read and write comments on any post via
  `/post/[id]`
- Tap-card-to-detail works for every post in the feed
- Scenario 20 demoable end-to-end
- The Comment primitive is in place for system-comment producers
  (BU-dispatch / BU-flag / BU-outcome-review) to add their own
  rows when they ship
- The `comment_added` analytics event is firing in development
- Feature flag `ff_comments` registered (per D036)
- The post-detail page becomes the first deep-linkable URL in the
  app — sets up D045's "share to social → land on detail" flow
  even before BU-share-out / BU-deep-link land

Next BU candidates (per the post-demo roadmap): BU-composer-fab
(D044) for richer composer experience, OR BU-attachments (Slice 2
full's last sub-BU), OR start the pilot path with BU-auth.
Decision belongs to Paul after comments ships.
